steps.title: Understanding average rate of change
steps.content: To find the greatest average rate of decrease, we calculate the temperature drop over each 10-minute interval: $$\text{Average rate} = \frac{\text{Temperature change}}{10 \text{ minutes}}$$ The most negative value indicates the steepest decline.

steps.title: Read temperatures from the graph
steps.content:
From the scatterplot, identify the temperature at each relevant time:
  • At 0 min: 200°F
  • At 10 min: 160°F
  • At 30 min: 130°F
  • At 40 min: 120°F
  • At 50 min: 110°F
  • At 60 min: 100°F
  • At 90 min: 80°F
  • At 100 min: 75°F

steps.title: Calculate average rate for each interval
steps.content:
Choice A (0–10 min): $\frac{160 - 200}{10} = \frac{-40}{10} = -4°F/\text{min}$
Choice B (30–40 min): $\frac{120 - 130}{10} = \frac{-10}{10} = -1°F/\text{min}$
Choice C (50–60 min): $\frac{100 - 110}{10} = \frac{-10}{10} = -1°F/\text{min}$
Choice D (90–100 min): $\frac{75 - 80}{10} = \frac{-5}{10} = -0.5°F/\text{min}$

The largest rate of decrease is −4°F/min between 0 and 10 minutes. That matches choice A — A is correct.
